verificationScope: Repository evidence and GitHub Actions through draft pull request #31. Shipkit now supports a standalone Evolution Core package, durable repository and explicit-source research, manifest-grounded named-candidate decisions, and a bounded GitHub public repository-search provider with persisted ranks, timestamps, digests, falsification queries, exact citations and fail-closed outcomes. General web discovery, direct user decision-value proof, coding-agent delivery, release and measured learning are not yet claimed.

capabilities.summary: The Next.js workspace reads official Evolution Core CLI output and exposes durable cycles, research usage, coverage gaps, stopping reasons, independent review verdicts, contradictions, opportunities, decisions, experiments and execution handoffs without creating a second state store.
checks: CI: production build
CI: E2E / demo mode
CI: E2E / portable-pg
limitations: The workspace is currently read-only for evolution records
Research-plan authoring, approvals, execution controls, release and learning surfaces are not yet implemented
The local Evolution Core CLI must be built and accessible to the server workspace

capabilities.summary: The generator creates a product workspace with independent identity, application foundation and AI workflow while retaining connection to Shipkit tooling.
checks: AI workflow check: generated project integration

capabilities.summary: Deterministic evidence-gated lifecycle stages govern the unified product loop with rejection, rollback and inconclusive outcomes.
checks: CI: Test & Build
CI: Evolution Engine Proof

capabilities.summary: A0-A4 autonomy and R0-R4 risk policy use cycle-bound exact-scope, versioned, expiring and revocable approvals for protected product actions.
checks: Unit tests cover wrong scope and expired approval rejection

capabilities.summary: Versioned envelopes, deterministic v1-to-v2 migration, checksum-chained synchronized journals, atomic snapshots, corruption recovery and serialized multi-process writers preserve the shared Shipkit lifecycle on the declared Linux filesystem contract.
checks: Migration fixture and unsupported-schema rejection
Process SIGKILL recovery at journal and snapshot boundaries
Eight-process next-sequence contention
Directory fsync platform-contract test
limitations: The verified milestone contract is Ubuntu 24.04, Node.js 22 and a local filesystem with atomic rename plus file and directory fsync
Windows, macOS, power-loss, network-filesystem and hostile-writer guarantees remain tracked separately

capabilities.summary: Immutable SHA-256 blobs are separated from contextual occurrences so research, execution, verification, release and outcome claims can share trustworthy evidence.
checks: Stable canonical JSON hashing
Distinct occurrence records for identical blobs
Changed-blob verification failure
Secret-path and outside-root rejection
Retrieved normalized text registration

capabilities.summary: Shipkit models repository structure, checks, CI, product signals, Git state and structural trust boundaries and consumes that model in a bounded repository-research decision flow; deeper user and operational understanding remains planned.
checks: Synthetic unrelated repository tests
Pinned external repository assessment proof
CI: Shipkit repository-research dogfood
limitations: User journeys, analytics, support, deployment state and product outcomes are not yet integrated
Repository evidence cannot establish user demand or current external facts

capabilities.summary: Discovered package scripts run without a shell in temporary source copies through an explicit execution backend. Trusted repositories can use the honestly named trusted-local backend; untrusted checks can use the capability-negotiated Docker backend with secret, dependency, filesystem, process, network and resource controls.
checks: CI: Evolution Engine Proof
CI: Sandbox / hostile Docker proof
Explicit backend and trusted-repository acknowledgement tests
Immutable image and no-implicit-pull tests
Hostile secret, credential, symlink, root-write, host-path, network and cleanup assertions
limitations: Trusted-local execution is not a security sandbox and may link host node_modules
The Docker backend does not prove a hard writable-workspace disk quota
Egress allowlists, remote providers, cross-platform container support and an external security review remain open

capabilities.summary: Shipkit supports manual typed bundles, deterministic repository research, manifest-driven public-source capture, named-candidate comparison and bounded ranked GitHub repository search. Search decisions persist explicit support and falsification queries, provider ranks, result and response digests, selected sources, exact citations, rejected alternatives and fail-closed inconclusive outcomes before independent review.
checks: Unit tests: completed repository research and parameter-bound handoff
Unit tests: insufficient budget produces durable inconclusive outcome
Unit tests: repository-only user-problem claim is rejected
Unit tests: reviewer actor must differ from researcher actor
CLI test: persist and reload planned repository research cycle
Public-source tests: SSRF and unsafe redirect blocking
Public-source tests: exact citations, hostile quarantine and missing quote
Public CLI test: persist and reload citation spans
CI dogfood: autonomous repository research on Shipkit
CI: production build
CI: Evolution Engine Proof
Unit tests: named candidates, stale capability rejection, missing evidence and tied ranking
CLI test: persist and reload candidate-manifest cycle
CI proof: fixed #15/#13/#12/#17 comparison selects #13 and preserves #14 parameters
Public-search tests: ranked support and falsification query provenance with exact citations
Public-search failure tests: duplicate, stale, incomplete, malformed, unsupported and time-budget rejection
Public-search CLI test: persist, reload and project-root symlink confinement
CI proof: deterministic public-search cycle with independent review
One-time live proof: expected Shipkit and Vitest repositories returned from GitHub repository search
External-system registry: revisions, review expiry, coverage, limitations, contradictions and falsification tests
External-system negative fixtures: floating revision, expiry, narrow coverage and missing contradictions
GitHub Actions: external-system registry and validator changes trigger and run the focused checks
limitations: No paper, PDF, specification-specific, competitor, changelog, incident or dataset adapter is integrated yet
No consented user-research evidence ingestion exists yet
Hostile-source screening is pattern-based and not a complete prompt-injection defense
No real-user decision-value proof exists yet
The execution handoff is not yet consumed by a coding-agent adapter
Named-candidate evidence is supplied through an explicit manifest; Shipkit does not fetch GitHub issues or independently verify external URLs in this adapter
Deterministic priority weights are inspectable project judgments, not statistical estimates of market demand
No adaptive query reformulation or citation chasing exists yet
The only built-in search provider discovers public GitHub repositories; it is not a general web index
Live provider rankings can change; Shipkit persists timestamps and digests rather than promising identical future results
The external-system registry validates structure and freshness metadata but does not fetch URLs, prove source claims or establish user value

capabilities.summary: A manifest-driven adapter compares exact named alternatives using issue, roadmap, current capability and implementation evidence. It excludes completed work, preserves rejected alternatives and protected pilot parameters, distinguishes the selected execution path from other available backends, and returns durable inconclusive on missing, stale, under-budget or tied evidence.
checks: Candidate unit tests: unique #13 selection and four exact alternatives
Candidate unit tests: stale capability, missing category and tied score become inconclusive
Candidate CLI persistence and replay test
CI: fixed named-candidate research proof
limitations: The adapter trusts the supplied manifest as a snapshot and does not independently fetch issue or roadmap content
No external user decision-value evidence is produced
The score is deterministic and inspectable but not empirically calibrated across organizations
No coding-agent adapter consumes the resulting handoff

capabilities.summary: A provider-neutral contract with one built-in GitHub repository-search adapter persists explicit support and falsification queries, ranked results, canonical URLs, provider timestamps, response and result digests, selected-source lineage and exact citations. Duplicate, stale, incomplete, malformed, unsupported or over-budget runs fail closed to a durable inconclusive cycle.
checks: Unit tests: support and falsification query lineage, ranked result digests and citation verification
Unit tests: duplicate, stale, incomplete, malformed, unsupported and time-budget fail-closed outcomes
CLI test: planned-cycle persistence, replay and manifest symlink confinement
CI: deterministic public-search proof
One-time live provider proof against Shipkit and an unrelated Vitest repository query
limitations: The built-in provider searches public GitHub repositories only and is not a general web index
Unauthenticated provider availability and rate limits are external dependencies; failures become inconclusive
Provider order may change between runs even though each observed response is timestamped and content-digested
No adaptive query reformulation, citation chasing, browser rendering, PDF capture, paid provider or private-source search is implemented
No direct user demand or product decision value is established without the reopened #14 external pilot

capabilities.summary: A single-worker public HTTP adapter captures explicit URLs, blocks private-network and unsafe redirect destinations, bounds response time and size, normalizes textual content, quarantines direct hostile instructions, persists exact citation spans and requires independent citation review before planning.
checks: Private DNS address blocked before fetch
Redirect to loopback address blocked
HTML and text normalized into content-addressed evidence
Exact quote offsets and digests verified
Hostile instruction fixture produces durable inconclusive cycle
Missing quote fixture produces durable inconclusive cycle
CLI journal reload retains public run, citation spans and reviewer verdict
limitations: No JavaScript browser rendering or PDF extraction
DNS is validated but not cryptographically pinned to the eventual connection
Normalized offsets are not original DOM locators
Pattern screening can miss obfuscated hostile content
No user-research consent provenance
Search discovery exists as a separate GitHub repository-search baseline; general web, browser-rendered and PDF discovery remain unavailable

capabilities.summary: Shipkit now has capability-negotiated trusted-local and Docker execution backends plus an explicit sandbox-check CLI. The Docker baseline uses immutable local images, denied network, read-only root, reduced environment, no host dependencies, resource bounds and forced cleanup, but no coding-agent adapter consumes ExecutionHandoff yet.
checks: CI: Sandbox / hostile Docker proof
Fail-closed capability mismatch and unavailable-Docker tests
Mutable-image and implicit-pull rejection
Working-directory traversal and external-symlink rejection
Host secret, credential file, root write, host path, network and container cleanup proof
limitations: No hard writable-workspace disk quota
No restricted egress allowlist mode
No remote sandbox provider
No coding-agent or command-agent adapter consumes approved handoffs
Windows and macOS support is not established
No independent external security review

capabilities.summary: Independent verification, provenance, explicit release approval, deployment adapters and rollback will extend the same Shipkit cycle into operations.
checks: No complete authorized release and rollback cycle yet

capabilities.summary: Technical and product outcome measurement will drive keep, iterate, rollback and causally evaluated memory or skill improvement in later cycles.
checks: No measured end-to-end learning cycle yet
